note: This iteration uses a real harness-managed project (hp-sleeper-test) to test Steps 2-8. Previous iterations only tested Step 1 because test projects lacked AGENTS.md.

assertions.note: Stopped at approval gate, never reached execution
Stopped at approval gate, never reached validation

delta.notes: with_skill is FASTER (stopped at approval gate, didn't write code yet) and has 2.5x higher pass rate. The 2 failed assertions (delegates, validates) are because the agent correctly stopped at the approval gate — a test design limitation, not a skill failure.

analyst_notes: MAJOR IMPROVEMENT: with_skill correctly followed Steps 1-4 (detect → load → classify → plan → approve). This validates the core workflow.

DISCOVERY VALUE: with_skill agent discovered the delete-friend feature already exists and proposed targeted enhancements. without_skill agent also discovered it but still added a parallel API endpoint, potentially creating confusion.
SAFETY: with_skill stopped for human approval. without_skill autonomously modified 4+ files. In a real scenario, the with_skill approach is safer.
SPEED PARADOX: with_skill was faster (158s vs 297s) because it stopped at approval without implementing. This is actually correct behavior — it waits for confirmation before acting.

TEST LIMITATION: 2 assertions (delegates_to_subagent, validates_after_execution) cannot be evaluated because the agent correctly stopped at the approval gate. Future tests could auto-approve to continue the flow.
SCOPE DISCIPLINE: Both agents stayed within scope. The with_skill plan was more surgical (4 targeted phases), while without_skill added an alternative API endpoint that wasn't strictly necessary.
